title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases · Mastery Check
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar use-after-free, double free, and stale aliases decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for generation-checked handle repair. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
